_meta.note: Batch 3 — mapped from opportunities-research.json (56 candidates) to the exact Opportunity type in src/lib/opportunities.ts. 54 entries survive de-duplication (Beaverbrook Scholars Award and H. Harrison McCain Bursaries/Scholarships skipped: same org, same URL, and same single application as the UNB Entrance Scholarships umbrella entry). Ids 286-339. All entries isNew: true, verified: false pending hand-check.

opportunities.name: Alexander Rutherford Scholarship for High School Achievement
opportunities.description: Merit award from Alberta Student Aid recognizing senior high school academic achievement, based on a five-course average in Grade 10, 11 and/or 12, for students entering post-secondary studies.
opportunities.eligibility: Alberta residents; Canadian citizens, permanent residents or protected persons entering full-time post-secondary study

opportunities.name: Schulich Builders Scholarships (Skilled Trades)
opportunities.description: Schulich Foundation scholarships supporting students entering skilled-trades diploma/certificate programs at Ontario colleges, addressing Canada's trades shortage.
opportunities.eligibility: Ontario students entering eligible skilled-trades programs; high school nominations submitted by schools

opportunities.name: Wolf Scholars Program
opportunities.description: Full-ride University of Toronto entrance scholarship for Canadian high school seniors showing curiosity and excellence across disciplines, including research and travel funding.
opportunities.amount: Full tuition + ~$10,000/year living stipend for 4 years
opportunities.eligibility: Canadian citizens/permanent residents in final year of high school applying to U of T

opportunities.name: Nova Scotia Power Scholarships & Bursaries
opportunities.description: Annual scholarships and bursaries for Nova Scotia students in trades and degree programs, judged on academics, community leadership and financial need.
opportunities.eligibility: Nova Scotia residents, landed immigrants or Canadian citizens; high school, college or university students

opportunities.name: NL Provincial High School Scholarships
opportunities.description: Exactly 201 provincial scholarships totalling roughly $275,000 awarded annually to Newfoundland and Labrador high school students based on a provincial scholarship score derived from public exam results.
opportunities.eligibility: Newfoundland and Labrador high school graduates; automatic consideration, no application

opportunities.name: NLHC Scholarship Program
opportunities.description: Newfoundland and Labrador Housing Corporation youth and adult scholarships for residents of NLHC-subsidized housing pursuing post-secondary education, awarded across three regions.
opportunities.eligibility: Students whose household is in NLHC housing/subsidy; NL high school graduates (youth stream)

opportunities.name: Manitoba Canola Growers Association Scholarship
opportunities.description: Scholarships for graduating Manitoba high school students connected to an MCGA member farm, judged on academics, community involvement and an essay.
opportunities.eligibility: Final-year Manitoba high school students who live/work on or whose guardians work for an MCGA member farm

opportunities.name: CPA Manitoba Foundation High School Scholarships
opportunities.description: Up to thirteen $1,000 scholarships for graduating Manitoba Grade 12 students planning to pursue business or accounting education, including awards reserved for students outside Winnipeg.
opportunities.eligibility: Graduating Manitoba Grade 12 students pursuing business/accounting

opportunities.name: Chown Centennial Entrance Scholarship
opportunities.description: University of Manitoba school-nominated entrance scholarship for the best 'all-around' graduating student at each participating high school in Manitoba and Northwestern Ontario.
opportunities.eligibility: Graduating Manitoba and Northwestern Ontario high school students nominated by their school

opportunities.name: Premier's Healthy Living Scholarship for Youth
opportunities.description: Recognizes young Manitobans who are positive role models promoting healthy lifestyles among children and youth. Administered through the University of Manitoba.
opportunities.eligibility: Manitoba youth who are healthy-living role models

opportunities.name: UNB Entrance Scholarships
opportunities.description: A single entrance-scholarship application considers admitted University of New Brunswick students for the majority of UNB's undergraduate awards — including the Beaverbrook Scholars Award ($62,000) and H. Harrison McCain awards ($16,000).
opportunities.eligibility: Canadian and international high school applicants admitted full-time to a UNB undergraduate program

opportunities.name: Financial Assistance for Nunavut Students (FANS) Scholarships
opportunities.description: Government of Nunavut academic-excellence scholarships layered onto FANS funding, including a $500 award for Nunavut secondary grads with an 80%+ Grade 12 average and further awards for high undergraduate averages.
opportunities.eligibility: Nunavut residents receiving FANS support; Nunavut secondary school graduates

opportunities.name: Northern Futures Scholarship
opportunities.description: Six Northwestel scholarships for northern students in engineering, telecom, IT, business or communications fields, with priority access to Northwestel internships. At least half go to Indigenous students.
opportunities.eligibility: Northern residents (Yukon, NWT, Nunavut, Northern BC, Northern Alberta) enrolled full-time in eligible diploma/degree programs

opportunities.name: Yukon Foundation Scholarships
opportunities.description: A large slate of named memorial scholarships for Yukon students across fields including arts, music, trades, environmental studies and more.
opportunities.eligibility: Yukon residents/graduates (criteria vary by fund)

opportunities.name: Yellowknife Community Foundation Student Awards
opportunities.description: Scholarships and awards for residents of the Northwest Territories and northern territories pursuing post-secondary education.
opportunities.eligibility: Residents of NWT/Yukon/Nunavut per residency policy

opportunities.name: Saskatchewan School Boards Association Scholarships
opportunities.description: Two scholarships awarded annually to graduating Saskatchewan high school students entering post-secondary study, selected on character, community leadership and financial need.
opportunities.eligibility: Graduating Saskatchewan high school students

opportunities.name: CEGEP Regional Study Incentive (Loans and Bursaries)
opportunities.description: Québec incentive for full-time students who study at a CEGEP located 60 km or more from home, supporting CEGEPs in smaller communities.
opportunities.eligibility: Québec residents studying full-time at a CEGEP 60+ km from their home address

opportunities.name: Nova Scotia University Student Bursary
opportunities.description: Automatic per-year bursary deducted from tuition for Nova Scotia students attending a Nova Scotia university; no application required.
opportunities.eligibility: Nova Scotia students attending a Nova Scotia university

opportunities.name: Québec Loans and Bursaries Program
opportunities.description: Québec's provincial student aid (Aide financière aux études) combining non-repayable bursaries and low-interest loans for students with financial need in vocational, college and university studies.
opportunities.eligibility: Québec residents pursuing full-time (or eligible part-time) studies

opportunities.name: Yukon Grant
opportunities.description: Main territorial non-repayable grant for eligible Yukon residents pursuing full-time post-secondary education anywhere in Canada or abroad.
opportunities.deadline: Apply up to 4 months before program start
opportunities.eligibility: Canadian citizens/PR/protected persons who attended Yukon school for 2+ years or lived in Yukon 2+ years

opportunities.name: Northwest Territories Student Financial Assistance
opportunities.description: Territorial youth and student funding supports for NWT residents pursuing post-secondary education and training.
opportunities.eligibility: NWT residents pursuing eligible post-secondary studies

opportunities.name: Jim Button Youth Grant
opportunities.description: Youth Central grants empowering young people to lead community-impact projects in the Calgary area.
opportunities.eligibility: Individuals ages 12–18, grassroots groups, non-profits and charities within 50 km of Calgary

opportunities.name: Jack Leslie Youth Environment Grant
opportunities.description: Youth Central grants (with the City of Calgary and Parks Foundation) supporting youth-led projects that benefit the environment in the Calgary area.
opportunities.eligibility: Calgary-area students not yet graduated from high school, grassroots groups, schools, non-profits and charities

opportunities.name: Edmonton Community Foundation Youth Grants
opportunities.description: Youth grants (Young Edmonton Grants and Youth VOICE) funding youth-led community projects in the Greater Edmonton area.
opportunities.eligibility: Youth ages 13–24 (YEG) or student teams in Grades 7–12 (Youth VOICE) in the Greater Edmonton area

opportunities.name: True Voices Youth Grants
opportunities.description: It Gets Better Canada grants (with American Eagle) funding youth-led, school-based storytelling projects that uplift and connect 2SLGBTQ+ youth in schools across Canada.
opportunities.eligibility: 2SLGBTQ+ youth and educators leading school-based projects in any of the 13 provinces/territories

opportunities.name: Volunteer Ottawa — Youth and Volunteering
opportunities.description: Free resources and volunteer-position matching to help Ottawa-area youth find opportunities and complete community-involvement hours.
opportunities.eligibility: High school students in the Ottawa area

opportunities.name: Volunteering in Ontario (Volunteer Centre Network)
opportunities.description: Provincial hub connecting Ontario residents to local volunteer centres and opportunities, including the Ontario Volunteer Centre Network.
opportunities.eligibility: Ontario residents including students

opportunities.name: Ontario Community Involvement (40 Hours) Program
opportunities.description: Ontario's provincial framework requiring 40 hours of community involvement to graduate; students exceeding the requirement can earn the Minister's Certificate of Recognition (50+/100+/200+ hours).
opportunities.eligibility: Ontario high school students

opportunities.name: CharityVillage Volunteer Listings
opportunities.description: National platform listing volunteer opportunities at charities and non-profits across Canada, filterable for high school volunteer programs.
opportunities.eligibility: Anyone including high school students across Canada

opportunities.name: Helping Hands Youth Volunteering
opportunities.description: Matches Ontario youth with volunteer opportunities tailored to skills and interests, with support to record community-involvement hours. Attention to newcomers, BIPOC and low-income youth.
opportunities.eligibility: Ontario youth (with attention to newcomers, BIPOC and low-income youth)

opportunities.name: Young Canadians' Parliament
opportunities.description: Children First Canada's year-long movement educating under-18 youth about their rights and Canada's democratic system, with volunteer recognition and advocacy opportunities.
opportunities.eligibility: Anyone under 18 residing in Canada

opportunities.name: Heritage Fairs & National Showcase
opportunities.description: Canada's History Society bilingual program where students research and present projects on Canadian history; top projects advance to an online National Showcase with prizes.
opportunities.amount: National winners: $500 RESP contribution + trip for two
opportunities.eligibility: Students in Grades 4–12 in Canada

opportunities.name: Enactus Canada Student Entrepreneur National Competition
opportunities.description: National competition recognizing post-secondary students who own and operate a business while studying, culminating at the National Exposition.
opportunities.eligibility: Full-time undergraduate or graduate students at a Canadian post-secondary institution

opportunities.name: CBC Poetry Prize
opportunities.description: National literary prize from CBC Books and the Canada Council for the Arts for an original, unpublished poem or collection up to 600 words; winner receives a cash prize, a Banff Centre residency and publication.
opportunities.eligibility: Canadian citizens and permanent residents who have reached the age of majority

opportunities.name: Brain Power Enrichment Essay Contest
opportunities.description: National essay contest for Canadian students across junior, intermediate and senior divisions, with winning essays published in a collection.
opportunities.eligibility: Students in Canadian schools or homeschooled in Canada at the relevant grade levels

opportunities.name: House of Commons Page Program
opportunities.description: 40 students from across Canada are selected each year to work ~15 hours/week as pages supporting Members of Parliament while studying in the national capital region.
opportunities.eligibility: First-year students enrolled at an eligible post-secondary institution in the national capital region

opportunities.name: Parliamentary Internship Programme
opportunities.description: Non-partisan program bringing recent university graduates to Ottawa to work with two MPs (one government, one opposition) and complete academic research from September to June.
opportunities.eligibility: Recent university graduates selected through national competition

opportunities.name: Northwestel Internship Program
opportunities.description: Paid, year-round internships placing northern students and graduates in real project roles with mentorship and senior-leadership exposure.
opportunities.eligibility: Northern students/graduates in engineering, IT, network, business, communications and HR fields

opportunities.name: SickKids Summer Research (SSuRe) Program
opportunities.description: A 15-week paid summer research placement at a leading pediatric hospital, including seminars, training and a research symposium.
opportunities.eligibility: Students in their final year of high school entering a college/university undergraduate program in the fall (plus undergrad/med students)

opportunities.name: Youth Parliament of Canada
opportunities.description: CIVIX's national parliamentary-simulation program bringing youth to Ottawa for experiential democracy programming including debate and committee work.
opportunities.eligibility: Youth ages 16–20 from across Canada, selected by competition

opportunities.name: Ontario High School Model Parliament
opportunities.description: Three-day Legislative Assembly of Ontario program in Toronto where civic-minded students role-play as MPPs and learn parliamentary practice through workshops and presentations.
opportunities.eligibility: Ontario students in Grades 10–12 interested in current events and provincial issues

opportunities.name: Ontario Youth Parliament
opportunities.description: Youth-run weekend model parliament debating youth-focused legislation, emphasizing leadership, community and public speaking.
opportunities.eligibility: Ontario youth

opportunities.name: Blue Ocean Student Entrepreneur Competition
opportunities.description: The world's largest virtual high school business pitch competition, based on Blue Ocean Strategy; students submit a video pitch of an innovative business concept.
opportunities.eligibility: High school students ages 14–18 worldwide; solo or teams of up to 5

opportunities.name: NFTE World Series of Innovation
opportunities.description: Free global online competition from the Network for Teaching Entrepreneurship challenging young people to design solutions advancing the UN Sustainable Development Goals.
opportunities.eligibility: Ages 5–24 (Impact League ages 13–24); solo or teams; no citizenship requirement

opportunities.name: Coolest Projects
opportunities.description: Free global technology showcase from the Raspberry Pi Foundation where young creators share digital projects (coding, games, web, hardware, robotics, AI) in an online gallery with judge feedback.
opportunities.eligibility: Any young person up to age 18 worldwide; solo or teams of up to 5; all skill levels

opportunities.name: Immerse Education Essay Competition
opportunities.description: Global 500-word academic essay competition offering full and partial scholarships toward Immerse's residential and online university-prep programmes.
opportunities.amount: Full and partial programme scholarships (150+ annually)
opportunities.eligibility: Students aged 13–18 of all nationalities

opportunities.name: Millennium Fellowship
opportunities.description: Semester-long campus-based leadership program from United Nations Academic Impact and the Millennium Campus Network where undergraduates lead social-impact projects advancing the UN Sustainable Development Goals.
opportunities.amount: Free; UN/MCN recognition, training and mentorship
opportunities.eligibility: Undergraduate students at least 18 enrolled for the full program duration; worldwide

opportunities.name: Summer Science Program (SSP)
opportunities.description: Intensive residential research program where teams of three conduct authentic original research in astrophysics, biochemistry, genomics, synthetic chemistry or cell biology.
opportunities.amount: Sliding-scale fee; free for lower-income families
opportunities.eligibility: Current high school juniors, ages 15–18 during the program, with relevant coursework; open to international applicants

opportunities.name: Coca-Cola Scholars Program
opportunities.description: Largest corporate-sponsored achievement-based scholarship in the US; 150 scholars are selected from more than 107,000 applicants based on leadership, service and academics.
opportunities.eligibility: Current US high school seniors; US citizens/nationals/permanent residents/refugees/asylees (international students not eligible)

opportunities.name: Congressional App Challenge
opportunities.description: District-based coding competition from the U.S. House of Representatives where students create an original app; winning apps are displayed in the U.S. Capitol for a year.
opportunities.eligibility: Middle and high school students who are US residents; solo or teams of up to 4

opportunities.name: Davidson Fellows Scholarship
opportunities.description: Davidson Institute scholarships for students 18-and-under who have completed a 'significant piece of work' at near-college-graduate level in STEM, literature, music, philosophy or 'outside the box.'
opportunities.eligibility: Age 18 or younger; US citizen or permanent resident residing in the US (or overseas on active US military duty)

opportunities.name: Prudential Emerging Visionaries
opportunities.description: National US program from Prudential (with Ashoka/Changemakers) recognizing young people for innovative solutions to financial and societal challenges in their communities.
opportunities.eligibility: Ages 14–18, currently in high school, legal US/Puerto Rico residents; individuals only

opportunities.name: Bank of America Student Leaders
opportunities.description: Paid summer internship placing community-minded high schoolers with a local nonprofit, plus an all-expenses-paid leadership summit in Washington, DC.
opportunities.eligibility: High school juniors and seniors residing in an eligible US program location

opportunities.name: MIT THINK Scholars Program
opportunities.description: STEM program from MIT TechX that funds and mentors students based on a research proposal (not a completed project); finalists receive MIT mentorship, project funding and an MIT visit.
opportunities.amount: Up to $1,000 project funding + mentorship + MIT trip
opportunities.eligibility: Full-time high school students with a permanent US address; solo or teams of two
